title: Is Delhi-NCR’s heatwave misery finally ending? IMD’s this-week forecast for Noida, Gurgaon and Ghaziabad mentions rain & storms
description: Delhi-NCR is witnessing a changing weather pattern between April 28 and April 30, with a yellow alert issued for rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds even as temperatures remain above 42°C. Dust storms have already been reported across parts of Delhi-NCR, though with varying intensity, as a cyclonic system moves towards the region. While heatwave conditions persist for now, forecasts suggest incoming pre-monsoon activity, including rain and strong winds, may bring gradual relief.

title: From Toxic Smog to Clear Skies: How Beijing Defeated Its Smog Crisis in Just a Decade | N18G
description: Beijing was once infamous for its deadly smog, with air pollution reaching hazardous levels during the 2013 environmental crisis. In response, China launched a massive, decade-long clean air campaign involving strict industrial regulations, expansion of electric vehicles, public transport upgrades, and a strong push toward renewable energy.By 2025, air quality in Beijing has improved significantly, marking some of the cleanest levels on record. However, pollution challenges still remain, with readings in some areas continuing to exceed WHO safety limits.
